etymology.description: 農(農) (nóng, "agriculture") + 民(民) (mín, "people") = people engaged in agriculture -> farmer or peasant
etymology.interpretation: 農民(農民) (nóngmín, "farmer") literally means 'people of agriculture,' referring to individuals whose livelihood is based on farming. Historically, peasants played a crucial role in maintaining agricultural productivity, and this term underscores their connection to the land and agricultural practices.

examples.chinese: 他是一个农民(他是一個農民)。
许多农民住在乡村(許多農民住在鄉村)。
examples.english: Many farmers live in the countryside.

examples.chinese: 春天到来时(春天到來時)，农民开始耕作(農民開始耕作)。
examples.pinyin: Chūntiān dàolái shí, nóngmín kāishǐ gēngzuò.
examples.english: When spring arrives, farmers begin to plow.
